title: Peppery Moroccan Lamb Zaalouk
description: A quick-fix, peppery moroccan dish featuring lamb leg, agemono-deep-fried with a blend of Mediterranean herbs, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This zaalouk is inspired by the flavors of North Africa, with a spicy kick from the preserved lemons and the earthiness of the cumin.
instructions: Cut the lamb leg into 1-inch cubes and season with salt and black pepper.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cumin, paprika, and red pepper flakes.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes to the skillet and sear until browned, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the skillet and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the skillet and saut9 the garlic and ginger until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the preserved lemons to the skillet and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Return the lamb cubes to the skillet and stir to combine with the garlic, ginger, and preserved lemons.
Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the lamb is cooked through.
Serve the lamb hot, garnished with fresh parsley and a sprinkle of black pepper.
